name: guayusa
mechanism: mechanism discussions focus on caffeine | theobromine | and polyphenol antioxidant pathways from leaf infusions
interactions: Potential additive stimulant burden with other caffeine-containing products or sympathomimetic drugs.
safetyNotes: Critical reviews report a documented history of beverage use, but product standardization and caffeine load still determine risk.
description: Guayusa (Ilex guayusa) is an Amazonian caffeinated leaf beverage plant with published reviews on composition and safe-use history.
dosage: No universally accepted therapeutic dosing standard is available; caffeine-equivalent intake context is necessary.
duration: No direct duration data. Contextual inference: Used in ritual, dreaming or folk medicine.. No direct mechanismofaction data. preparation: Commonly prepared as a brewed leaf infusion rather than concentrated pharmaceutical extracts.
region: Native use is concentrated in Ecuadorian and broader Amazonian regions.
traditionalUse: Traditional Amazonian communities use guayusa infusions in social and early-morning ritual contexts.
